programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: The Mayor loses his assistant, Ms. Bellum.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: Buttercup steals Octi to teach Bubbles a lesson.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.desc: Duncan goes on a mission to scare Courtney during a field trip to the museum.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: The girls rally behind Susan when her mother announces she will be leaving and that this will be Susan's last day at Malory Towers! Clarissa and Ruth have a plan, but soon find themselves in hot water.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: When the farmer gets stuck in the Loo, Bitzer must urgently deliver vital supplies. His initial efforts end in failure, so he drafts in Shaun and the gang to help out.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: The gang team up with Sherlock Holmes in London.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: Buttercup is knocked off-kilter by a mysterious force.

programme.title: Open Season: Call of Nature
programme.sub-title: Will the Real McSquizzy Please Stand Up?
programme.desc: A bump on the head turns McSquizzy into a kinder, gentler squirrel - a development which Elliot is more than happy to exploit.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin is too polite to speak his mind, so Gumball teaches him to be direct via an epic battle of insults.

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: Oscar and Hedgehog must work with the witches to cure Alice's camp-threatening illness.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Inspired by the \.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: To allow Alix to celebrate her birthday with her friends and family, Marinette gets the Rabbit Miraculous back and becomes the Keeper of Time for a few hours.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: Hawkodile's caught by the Action Police for bodyguarding without an Action License. He can't protect the kingdom until he passes the test at the DMV, but it's for nerds!

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.sub-title: Roy is Crocalor, and Crocalor is Roy!
programme.desc: Roy and Crocalor accidentally switch places while exploring a festival in Kitakami.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.sub-title: Tinkatink's Hammer Wasn't Made in a Day
programme.desc: Tinkatink puts up a fight when a wild Bombirdier tries to mess with its beloved hammer.

programme.title: Good Game: Spawn Squad
programme.sub-title: Louie and Harry Don't Win Mario Kart!?
programme.desc: Louie and Harry compete in 3 chaotic Mario Kart World challenges! From crazy coin battles to a Grand Prix where they don't want to win - will they be Super Stars or will the two of them Bo-bomb?

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: To solve the mystery of a ghostly performer haunting her former venue, the Gang has to pretend to be a rock band themselves. But can they solve the case before Velma has to actually get on stage and sing?

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.sub-title: Say Hello to my Little Friends
programme.desc: After telling the kids he will not miss them over the long weekend, Chef locks himself into the school and finds out he was very, very wrong.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.desc: The hottest day of the year and a broken air conditioner leads to a water battle of epic proportions to determine who controls the garden hose.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Tobias's sister, Rachel, invites Gumball's class to her high school party, requiring each kid to bring a date.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin trick Larry into giving them a video game refund.

programme.title: Steven Universe
programme.desc: Steven joins an underground wrestling league with Amethyst, who is tired of Pearl and Garnet bringing her down.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: Bubbles learns to embrace her \.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: When Hawkodile develops a secret crush on Dr. Fox, he finds himself actually crushing down in size!

programme.title: Pokemon: Ruby and Sapphire
programme.desc: Ash and friends meet a talented Trainer who performs a ribbon dance with her Mawile... until Team Rocket gets in the way!

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Today's secret mission sees Phil escaping the rat race and heading for the country - helping two rural properties struggling to sell.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: A month into his job, Ian is still trying to find somewhere to sit. But there are more important issues to get to grips with - not least the director general's desire to shape the debate as to what the BBC is really for.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Helen is adjusting to James being away and decides to move home to help her family. Mrs Hall is pulled in two directions and must choose one. Siegfried struggles to keep Skeldale House together.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.sub-title: The Hound of the Cancer Cells
programme.desc: When a famous cancer researcher is found dead, Holmes and Watson must determine if the scientist's untimely demise was tied to his latest cutting-edge invention.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Elizabeth operates on a man with a flesh-eating infection, Pratt takes offense at the Halloween costumes he sees in the ER, a new intern has a daunting physical handicap.

programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Food icon Donna Hay is in the house and it's all lemons all the time with a delicious fettuccine followed by a dessert that is a guaranteed dinner party showstopper.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Award-winning host Stephen Fry wrangles a stellar panel of comedians in the world's most impossible quiz show. This episode features Ben Miller, Sean Lock, Rob Brydon and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Chris Hoy, Ronni Ancona, Danny Wallace & Gabby Logan
programme.desc: Chris Hoy, Ronni Ancona, Danny Wallace and Gabby Logan join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: The MythBusters discuss 12 favourite myths involving forms of transportation.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil is in Birmingham where he's trying to sell two tricky terraces on the same street with completely different problems. In a crammed market, can he help these houses stand out?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: A flying instructor and his hovercraft instructor and trapeze artist partner build an incredible metal sculptural home next to their very own airfield in Scotland.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Damien Leith, Andrew McClelland, Fernando Saunders & Meshel Laurie
programme.desc: Joining Adam Hills are Damien Leith, Fernando Saunders, Andrew McClelland and Meshel Laurie.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Join Sandi Toksvig for an Upsadaisy with Justin Moorhouse, Emmanuel Sonubi, Holly Walsh and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material from the comedy panel show. Over the course of the show, celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. But are they true?

programme.title: Tonight At The Museum
programme.desc: An after-hours adventure at the museum as Alex Lee welcomes Pete Helliar, Danielle Walker, Alex Ward and Briggs for laughs and exhibition glory.

programme.title: Gruen
programme.desc: Woolies tries a fresh tactic, Lynx still stinks and an ad that celebrates your bond with a roof. Plus a pitch to get Gen Z to watch TV. Wil is joined by Todd Sampson, Jasmin Bedir, Karen Ferry and Russel Howcroft.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: The news has never been more of a mess, but Charlie Pickering tries to make sense of the nonsense. We watch the week so that you don't have to. With special guests Rhys Nicholson, Nicolette Minster and Felicity Ward.

programme.title: Planet America
programme.desc: Chas and John bring you the latest on the fragile ceasefire deal between the US and Iran. Donald Trump suspended bombing and attacks on Iran for two weeks of negotiations. Is this just a temporary pause or a path to peace?

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Elizabeth clashes with the new surgical resident over a patient's care. Tension mounts between nurses and doctors as a petition against Luka leaves the ER short-staffed.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: A month into his job, Ian is still trying to find somewhere to sit. But there are more important issues to get to grips with - not least the director general's desire to shape the debate as to what the BBC is really for.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Count down the hottest chart hits and catch up on some of the most promising new popular music videos on rage.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: Charlie brings you all the news from the March for Justice to the search for the Thylacine; Margaret Pomeranz reviews; what has Kitty Flanagan been up to? If you know, please alert the authorities.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: The flood of pixelated scandals, the actual flood, and can an app solve society's problems? Plus, Zoe Coombs Marr investigates Lesbians in the Workplace, and Andy Zaltzman. He's also on the show. Not being investigated.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: A ship stuck in the Suez, the Prime Minister is rearranging deck chairs, an MP is reaching out to his constituents from the bushes, Nazeem Hussain is saving the saviour, and Nath Valvo is stuck in the TV.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Join Sandi Toksvig for a QI look at Veggies with Jason Manford, Ahir Shah, Holly Walsh, and Alan Davies.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Award-winning host Stephen Fry wrangles a stellar panel of comedians in the world's most impossible quiz show. This episode features Ben Miller, Sean Lock, Rob Brydon and Alan Davies.

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors get to grips with guts when Dr Xand swallows a camera pill to show the incredible villi inside his intestines which absorb the nutrients from food.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors explain that humans are mostly made of water. They say hello to the hypothalamus, the amazing part of the brain that keeps people hydrated.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven's supervillain father comes to visit and the rest of the team thinks he's the coolest... until he steals their souls.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg and Beast Boy create clones of themselves to do their chores and the clones create more clones until the situation grows out of control.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.desc: Harold's world is turned upside down when his classmates declare Beth is the real class ninja.

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: Felicity is on track to win the school cross country race, but when she fails to cross the finish line after a clash with Alicia, Darrell fears the worst and goes in search of her - with dramatic consequences...

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The farmer is stressed out and has had enough of the farm and its inhabitants. He storms off for a break, suitcase in hand, only to reappear almost immediately, the same, but somehow different.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: The gang help Ricky Gervais with a haunted statue.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: When an ancient prophecy spells doom for Townsville, the girls must race to stop it.

programme.title: Open Season: Call of Nature
programme.desc: Boog invents a new barter system using some shiny yellow nuggets he found as a currency.

programme.title: Summer Camp Island
programme.desc: Oscar and Hedgehog must learn to exist without each other when they are put on different teams during a scavenger hunt.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin make friends with classmate Clayton, a compulsive liar who can change shape.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Following the destruction of their tower by Slade, the Titans go house hunting.

programme.title: Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir
programme.desc: Marinette wants to find a magical solution for her friend's phobia, but not all problems can be solved by Ladybug.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: When Unikitty and the gang discover a gross bug in the castle, they desperately want it gone for good, but Unikitty refuses to allow them to squish it, no matter how icky or dangerous looking it is.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: Encounters at the Crystal Pool lead to battles, new friends, and discoveries about the past.

programme.title: Pokemon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: Joined by Perrin, the Rising Volt Tacklers set out to find Kleavor on Tengu Mountain.

programme.title: Good Game: Spawn Squad
programme.sub-title: Nic Takes on a Dragon in Rayman Legends!
programme.desc: Nic shows Harry the ropes in Rayman Legends! It's a masterclass as they play through time trials, controller swapping and end on an epic challenge - Dragon Slayer! Do they have what it takes to take that pesky dragon down?

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Shaggy and Scooby's favourite chef is finally opening his own restaurant but a monstrous Yeti threatens the opening night.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.desc: Cody is set to inherit a video game empire from his long-lost uncle, but only if he and his friends have what it takes to conquer a fantastical live action video game.

programme.title: Total DramaRama
programme.desc: When the city experiences a toilet paper shortage, Beth and Harold investigate and discover it's their old rival Sewer Mike who is the mastermind behind it all.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball discovers that Bobert is planning to take over Gumball's identity and replace him.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Gumball's class goes on a picnic, Gumball and Darwin get separated from the class and lost in the evil forest, where they have to survive.

programme.title: Steven Universe
programme.desc: During a mission with the Gems, Steven finds and befriends a magical lion in the desert.

programme.title: The Powerpuff Girls
programme.desc: Buttercup and Bubbles \.

programme.title: Unikitty
programme.desc: When Puppycorn uses a wishing well and it doesn't come true, Unikitty is shocked to discover the reason: all the wishes made over the years can't be granted because they're trapped at the bottom of the well.

programme.title: Pokemon: Ruby and Sapphire
programme.desc: To help a local researcher, our heroes explore a massive underground labyrinth created by a group of Trapinch... and get into some trouble along the way!

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil is in Birmingham where he's trying to sell two tricky terraces on the same street with completely different problems. In a crammed market, can he help these houses stand out?

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: A national paper has not only published details of Ian Fletcher's salary but followed up with a story of how he took his former PA to Italy. The BBC try to pre-empt criticism by persuading him to cut his own salary.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: It's Christmas, and James is miles away at the RAF training base. He's determined to get back to Helen, but Georgie needs his help. James struggles to do the right thing. Will he find his way home in time?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: As our team start to investigate a confounding murder, they discover a family with numerous skeletons in their closets. And an old face returns to St Marie - but isn't met with open arms from everyone on the team.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Elizabeth clashes with the new surgical resident over a patient's care. Tension mounts between nurses and doctors as a petition against Luka leaves the ER short-staffed.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: This episode QI Master Stephen Fry is joined by panellists Alan Davies, Phill Jupitus, Jack Dee and Ross Noble to explore the higgledy piggledy theme of 'Hodge Podge'.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine & Esme Young
programme.desc: The guests joining Lee Mack and David Mitchell are Tom Allen, Geoff Norcott, Vicki Pepperdine and Esme Young.

programme.title: MythBusters
programme.desc: The MythBusters count down their 12 favourite water-based myths from prior episodes.

programme.title: Phil Spencer: Secret Agent
programme.desc: Phil visits Billericay in Essex, where Julie and her four sons are struggling to keep up with their demanding home.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: This episode brings Japanese design to the Welsh valleys.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Spicks and Specks
programme.sub-title: Lisa Kekaula, Terry Psiakis, Guy Noble & Hamish Blake
programme.desc: Joining Adam, Myf and Alan tonight are Lisa Kekaula (from The Bellrays), Guy Noble, Hamish Blake and Terry Psiakis.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry is joined by Alan Davies, Reverend Richard Coles, Victoria Coren and Sue Perkins to discuss Knights and Knaves.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Bez, Motsi Mabuse, Shazia Mirza & Steve Pemberton
programme.desc: Rob Brydon sits in the host's chair, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell as the team captains.

programme.title: Ghost
programme.desc: A ghost teams up with a psychic to uncover the truth behind his murder - and to rescue his sweetheart from a similar fate.

programme.title: ER
programme.desc: Abby fears the worst when her brother Eric suddenly disappears, while Carter and Chen suspect a frightened Chinese immigrant nanny was impregnated by her employer.

programme.title: W1A
programme.desc: A national paper has not only published details of Ian Fletcher's salary but followed up with a story of how he took his former PA to Italy. The BBC try to pre-empt criticism by persuading him to cut his own salary.

programme.title: Like a Version
programme.desc: triple j's legendary Like A Version segment hosts the greatest local and international acts, performing a cover of a song they love, and one of their own.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.

programme.title: The Set
programme.desc: Join Linda Marigliano and Dylan Alcott at their house party. Lime Cordiale and Miiesha play their hits including On Our Own and Twisting Words, plus a special one-off collab in front of a live crowd.

programme.title: The Set
programme.sub-title: Peking Duk, Jesswar and The Amity Affliction
programme.desc: Peking Duk, Jesswar and The Amity Affliction play their hits including Stranger, Soak Me in Bleach and Medusa, plus a one-off collab in front of a live crowd.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'quacks' and 'quackery', with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Victoria Coren Mitchell, Stephen K Amos, and Claudia Winkleman.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: This episode QI Master Stephen Fry is joined by panellists Alan Davies, Phill Jupitus, Jack Dee and Ross Noble to explore the higgledy piggledy theme of 'Hodge Podge'.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie, along with guest performer Jonathan Mangum.

programme.title: ABC News Update
programme.desc: The news headlines updating the day's top stories from across Australia and around the world.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Richard Osman hosts as presenter Kelly Cates, actor Tyger Drew-Honey, comedian Desiree Burch and journalist Matt Allwright test their general knowledge skills.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors investigate how enzymes speed up reactions inside the body with lots of foam. Dr Xand explores how music affects the brain when he visits a special orchestra.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: A look at the body's enormous, explosive, energy storage system, the liver; Lilia undergoes an amazing operation on her brain.
